_doc: First-slice WorldManifest for the Claude Code host (PLAN.md E13.2). Drives the PreToolUse gate in .claude/hooks/world-gate.py. FAIL-OPEN and ADDITIVE: anything not matched is allowed, and the gate never auto-approves (it only returns deny/ask), so it cannot weaken your existing permission prompts.
_projected_tools_doc: null = allow all native tools (ABSENT-for-native is OFF — the safe default). Set to a list, e.g. ["Read","Grep","Glob"], to project a restricted surface; native tools outside it become ABSENT (denied as 'not in this world'). MCP and control tools are never touched.
_trust_pins_doc: Operator attestations that a specific source is trusted, pinned to its content identity (DECISIONS D29, docs/trust-pins.md). A Read of a matching path whose live content still matches `identity` is classified Trusted and does NOT taint; any drift (hash/commit change) revokes the pin and the read taints as normal. Pins never weaken the egress floor (invariant 7) — they re-classify a vouched source upstream of taint, they do not let tainted data egress. identity.kind: 'sha256' (file bytes) or 'git_commit' (a reference repo's own HEAD with a clean tree).

trust_pins.reason: Reviewed AGT README for the repos/3p adoption analysis (docs/THIRD-PARTY-ADOPTION.md, DECISIONS D27).
